questions.question: Explain the significance of exit status 0 in shell scripting and list three common non-zero exit codes with their meanings.
keyPoints: Exit status 0 indicates successful completion of a command
Common non-zero codes: 1 (general error), 2 (misuse of command), 126 (command cannot execute)
127 (command not found), 128+N (script terminated by signal N)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the $? variable in shell scripting? When should it be checked?
keyPoints: $? holds the exit status of the most recently executed foreground command
It should be checked immediately after the command as it gets overwritten by each subsequent command
Values: 0 = success, non-zero = failure

questions.question: Write a bash script snippet that checks if a file exists and exits with appropriate status codes.
keyPoints: Use test or [ command to check file existence
Exit with 0 if file exists, 1 if not
Proper conditional structure with exit commands

questions.question: Explain the EXIT trap in bash and give a practical use case.
keyPoints: EXIT trap executes when the shell script terminates
Defined using: trap 'commands' EXIT
Useful for cleanup: deleting temp files, closing file descriptors, logging completion

questions.question: What is the difference between 'return' and 'exit' in shell functions?
keyPoints: return - exits only the function, returns control to the calling script with an exit status
exit - terminates the entire script immediately
return cannot be used outside a function; exit can be used anywhere

questions.question: Explain the default behavior of exit status in pipelines and how 'set -o pipefail' changes it.
keyPoints: Default: pipeline returns exit status of the LAST command only
set -o pipefail: pipeline returns exit status of rightmost (last) command that failed
If all commands succeed, pipeline returns 0

questions.question: What will be the exit status of the script if the following command fails: 'grep pattern file.txt' when the pattern is not found?
keyPoints: grep returns exit status 1 when no matches are found
Exit status 1 indicates the pattern was not found in the file
This can be used in conditional statements like if grep fails

questions.question: Write a complete bash script that backs up a directory, handles errors appropriately using exit status codes, and uses an EXIT trap for cleanup. Include comments explaining error handling.
keyPoints: Script accepts source and destination directories as arguments
Validates arguments (check if source exists)
Uses appropriate exit codes: 1 for general error, 2 for invalid usage
Implements tar command for backup with error checking
EXIT trap to remove any temporary files if script fails
Returns 0 on successful backup, non-zero on failure

questions.question: Explain the relationship between signal numbers and exit codes. How would you identify if a script was terminated by Ctrl+C? Provide examples.
keyPoints: Exit code = 128 + signal number when process is terminated by a signal
SIGINT (Ctrl+C) is signal 2, so exit code = 128+2 = 130
Check $? value in a wrapper script or signal handler
Examples: SIGKILL(9)=137, SIGHUP(1)=129, SIGTERM(15)=143

questions.question: Design a menu-driven shell script that performs different operations (create file, delete file, list files) with proper exit status handling. Explain how you would test each exit path.
keyPoints: Create menu using select or case statement
Each operation returns appropriate exit codes: 0=success, 1=error
Test successful operations: echo $? should be 0
Test failure cases: missing file, permission denied
Document expected exit codes for each operation
Use trap for cleanup on unexpected exit

questions.question: Explain how exit status is used in conditional execution with && and || operators. Write a script demonstrating proper error propagation.
keyPoints: && executes next command only if previous succeeded ($?=0)
|| executes next command only if previous failed ($?≠0)
Combined usage: command1 && command2 || command3 for error handling
Exit status can be propagated using exit $? to parent processes
Demonstrate with example: cp file1 file2 && echo 'Success' || echo 'Failed'
